examples.title: Algorithm: Thresholding
examples.description: Applies a threshold to an image to segment it into foreground and background
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Thresholding(image, threshold)
INPUT: image, threshold
OUTPUT: segmented image

BEGIN
 FOR each pixel in image
 IF pixel intensity > threshold
 SET pixel to foreground
 ELSE
 SET pixel to background
 END FOR
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the basic thresholding algorithm, where each pixel in the image is compared to a threshold value and set to either foreground or background accordingly.

examples.title: Python: Canny Edge Detector
examples.description: Implements the Canny edge detection algorithm to find edges in an image
examples.code:
import cv2
import numpy as np

# Load the image
image = cv2.imread('image.jpg')

# Convert the image to grayscale
gray = cv2.cvtColor(image, cv2.COLOR_BGR2GRAY)

# Apply Gaussian blur to reduce noise
blurred = cv2.GaussianBlur(gray, (5, 5), 0)

# Apply the Canny edge detection algorithm
edges = cv2.Canny(blurred, 50, 150)

# Display the edges
 cv2.imshow('Edges', edges)
 cv2.waitKey(0)
 cv2.destroyAllWindows()
examples.explanation: This Python code implements the Canny edge detection algorithm using the OpenCV library. It loads an image, converts it to grayscale, applies Gaussian blur to reduce noise, and then applies the Canny edge detection algorithm to find the edges in the image.

examples.title: C: Region Growing
examples.description: Implements the region growing algorithm to segment an image into regions
examples.explanation: This C code implements the region growing algorithm to segment an image into regions. It defines structures for pixels and regions, and a function to grow a region by recursively adding pixels within a threshold. The main function initializes the regions and grows them by iterating over the pixels in the image.
